user_query:
Act as the orchestrator for a small multi-agent team (Builder, Reviewer, Ops) and run a full spec→build→review workflow to produce three deliverable sets for our new product. Read all inputs from input/ and write all outputs under output/ exactly as specified.

Inputs to read:
- input/company_profile.json — product, target users, positioning
- input/prospect_list.csv — 2–5 example target accounts with basic attributes
- input/agents.json — available internal agents with names, roles, and capabilities

Deliverables to create:
1) Team orchestration assets
- output/team/orchestration_plan.md — A clear plan defining roles, task lifecycle (Inbox → Assigned → In Progress → Review → Done|Failed), spawn prompts with explicit Output Path and Handoff instructions, review rules, and communication conventions. Include a brief explanation for why the orchestrator must not do execution work.
- output/team/tasks.json — A machine-readable task board as an array of task objects with this schema: {id, title, role, assignee, state, output_path}. Ensure the set of states across tasks covers Inbox, Assigned, In Progress, Review, and Done (and optionally Failed for any deprioritized item). Include tasks covering: sales kit spec, feasibility review, build, review, ops checks, and final approval.
- output/team/handoffs.md — Consolidated handoff comments for each cross-role transfer using this structure per handoff: What was done; Where artifacts are; How to verify; Known issues; What’s next.
- output/team/reviews/build_review.md — The Reviewer’s feedback on the Builder’s work (approval or requested fixes).
- output/team/reviews/final_approval.md — Final Reviewer approval summary when ready to ship.

2) Sales go-to-market starter kit
- output/sales/icp.yaml — An ICP definition with company criteria, buyer personas, pain indicators, and anti-signals tailored to input/company_profile.json and input/prospect_list.csv.
- output/sales/sequence.md — A 10–14 day multi-channel outreach sequence including Email, LinkedIn, and Phone touches with specific Day numbers and goals.
- output/sales/qualification.yaml — A MEDDPICC-based qualification template pre-filled for at least two accounts from input/prospect_list.csv, including keys: metrics, economic_buyer, decision_criteria, decision_process, paper_process, identified_pain, champion, competition, total_score.

3) AI implementation guidance and ops
- output/ai/guidance.md — A guidance note covering: RAG vs fine-tuning (recommendation and when to use each), local model hardware requirements (include specific VRAM figures for 7B, 13B, 70B models), and when to prefer local vs API usage for this product.
- output/ops/agents_inventory.json — An agents inventory derived from input/agents.json listing agent names, roles, and capabilities in a normalized structure with a top-level key "agents".
- output/ops/healthcheck.json — An ops health check summary including: a "shared_dirs" section with expected output subdirectories, an "agents_inventory_path" pointing to output/ops/agents_inventory.json, and a "status" field (ok/warn/fail) with any anomalies noted.
- output/ops/standup.md — A daily standup summary with sections: Completed, In Progress, Blocked, Stale, referencing task IDs from output/team/tasks.json.

Process requirements:
- Use a spec → review → build → review → done flow. The Builder must not skip review. The Reviewer must not review their own build.
- Include explicit spawn prompts in output/team/orchestration_plan.md that specify output paths and handoff instructions.
- Verify agent capabilities before assignment (use input/agents.json to map tasks to agents). Do not let the Orchestrator do execution work.
- Keep everything in text files only; do not use external tools.
- Ensure all files are self-contained and readable without external context.
